data.title: Hyperliquid's protocol revenue in the past 24 hours surpassed pump.fun
data.description: According to Defillama data, on September 19, Hyperliquid's protocol revenue in the past 24 hours reached US$2.75 million, surpassing pump.fun's US$1.83 million and second only to Tether ($21.83 million) and Circle ($7.75 million).

data.title: PayPal's stablecoin PYUSD0 is now available on the Sei Network
data.description: PayPal's stablecoin, PYUSD 0, has officially launched on the Sei Network, further expanding its cross-chain application scenarios and supporting more on-chain payments and settlements. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Grayscale Crypto 5 ETF officially listed on NYSE Arca
data.description: Grayscale announced that its CoinDesk Crypto 5 ETF has been listed on the New York Stock Exchange Arca under the ticker symbol GDLC. Formerly known as the Grayscale Digital Large Cap Fund, this multi-asset cryptocurrency ETP covers five major cryptocurrencies: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, XRP, and Cardano. The fund, which is rebalanced quarterly, aims to track the leading assets by market capitalization and liquidity, and is based on the CoinDesk 5 Index developed by CoinDesk Indices.

data.title: U.S. Judge Dismisses Trump's $15 Billion Lawsuit Against The New York Times
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, a U.S. judge has dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump's $15 billion lawsuit against The New York Times, allowing him to amend his complaint. The judge ruled that Trump's filing violated federal rules, requiring a concise statement to justify the plaintiff's entitlement to damages. The judge emphasized that the complaint should not serve as a public forum for insults or a protected platform for attacking opponents.

Previously, on September 16, President Trump filed a defamation lawsuit against The New York Times. He claimed that the publication's reports harmed his reputation, business interests, Truth Social, and his TRUMP cryptocurrency project.

data.title: CFTC Announces New Members for Global Markets Advisory Committee
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) Acting Chair Caroline D. Pham has announced new members for the Global Markets Advisory Committee (GMAC) and its subcommittees. Scott Lucas, Head of Digital Assets Markets at JPMorgan and Managing Director, has been appointed as Co-Chair of the GMAC Digital Assets Markets Subcommittee. He will serve alongside Sandy Kaul, Executive Vice President at Franklin Templeton.

data.title: Trump says call with Chinese president goes well, will meet during APEC summit
data.description: US President Trump tweeted that he had a "very productive" call with his Chinese counterpart, achieving progress on key issues including trade, the fentanyl issue, the Russia-Ukraine conflict, and the approval of the TikTok transaction. The two sides agreed to meet at the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) summit in South Korea and plan to exchange visits. Trump called the call "very smooth" and said they would maintain telephone contact and look forward to meeting again at the APEC summit.

data.title: The U.S. Treasury Department has initiated the drafting of the GENIUS Act and is soliciting opinions on stablecoin regulation.
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department recently launched the Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (ANPRM) process to formally implement the GENIUS Act (U.S. Stablecoin National Innovation Guidance and Establishment Act), signed by President Trump. This marks the beginning of the substantive regulatory phase for stablecoin legislation. The Treasury Department is soliciting comments on details such as issuer restrictions, sanctions compliance, anti-money laundering requirements, the balance between federal and state regulation, tax issues, and custody of reserve assets. The public and crypto companies can submit feedback by October 20th. The GENIUS Act is the first crypto legislation to become law, and the Treasury Department's move aims to further refine the regulatory framework and complement the currently under discussion Digital Asset Market Clarity Act. (CoinDesk)

data.title: BNB Rockets 999,900% to $1K – Can Binance Coin Smash New Highs by Sept 30?
data.description:

This meteoric rise raises the question: can Binance Coin continue to gain momentum and potentially set new record highs by the end of September? Investors and enthusiasts alike are eagerly speculating on this possibility, analyzing market trends and performance indicators to determine the future trajectory of this popular digital asset.

data.title: The U.S. House of Representatives passed a temporary spending bill, and the Senate may trigger a fierce confrontation
data.description: The U.S. House of Representatives passed a temporary spending bill aimed at averting a federal government shutdown on October 1st, but this also sets up a fierce clash in the Senate later on Friday. Democrats are expected to reject the bill on the grounds that "healthcare spending needs to be increased." House Speaker Johnson overcame the dissatisfaction of some Republican colleagues and pushed the bill through with a vote of 217 in favor and 212 against. House Democrats voted almost unanimously against the bill. The Senate plans to simultaneously consider the bill submitted by the House and the "alternative version of the temporary spending bill" proposed by Democratic leader Schumer (which plans to provide funding for the government until October 31st) later on Friday. Both versions of the bill are expected to be rejected, which means that the U.S. government will be stuck in a shutdown crisis less than two weeks before the October 1st deadline. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Treasury Seeks Public Input on GENIUS Act Preliminary Rules
data.description: According to Foresight News, the U.S. Treasury Department has initiated a public comment period for the preliminary rule-making steps of the GENIUS Act. This period aims to gather information that will assist in developing more detailed proposals in the future. The deadline for submitting comments is October 20.
